mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below -0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 190, aggressive Fed rate hikes fail to anchor inflation expectations, causing real rates to spike and compress equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline significantly when CPIAUCSL crosses 190, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for real yield spreads.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precede positive corporate earnings revisions and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew during housing upcycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand that supports wage growth and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative in > 55% of months where payroll growth exceeds 200k, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real estate financing and stable industrial output indicates credit-fueled demand expansion that lifts cyclical equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain variance in forward returns beyond a baseline model, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained periods where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts, capacity bottlenecks sustain corporate pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate > HOUST growth rate
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when the INDPRO/HOUST ratio exceeds 1.05, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during demand-driven recoveries.
